source_key: 8.1 Multiplying by -1 is a geometric action
title: Multiplying by -1 is a geometric action
role: Give -1 a concrete transformation meaning: a half-turn or reversal through the origin.
prompt_core: Show a point or arrow on a number line being carried through the origin to the opposite side by a clean 180-degree half-turn arc. The object’s magnitude stays intact while orientation reverses. The image should feel crisp and almost ceremonial, like the simplest nontrivial symmetry in the book. Midnight palette, silver linework, no textual clutter.
caption: Multiplying by negative one is the geometry of reversal.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "8.1 Multiplying by -1 is a geometric action".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Give -1 a concrete transformation meaning: a half-turn or reversal through the origin.

Layout intent:
H — Half-page conceptual plate

Style direction:
half-turn symmetry plate

Core image brief:
Show a point or arrow on a number line being carried through the origin to the opposite side by a clean 180-degree half-turn arc. The object’s magnitude stays intact while orientation reverses. The image should feel crisp and almost ceremonial, like the simplest nontrivial symmetry in the book. Midnight palette, silver linework, no textual clutter.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
Multiplying by negative one is the geometry of reversal.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: What (-a)b means
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Separate magnitude of scaling from orientation reversal.
prompt_core: Compose an image where a scale factor of magnitude a acts on b, but a second visual layer adds a flip through the origin. Show that the amount of resizing and the orientation reversal are distinct ingredients that can be composed in either order on the line. Clean transformation sequence, strong visual hierarchy, optional overlay for arrows and signs.
caption: A negative factor means scale, then reverse orientation.
overlay: Overlay small arrow annotations if needed.

title: Squares forget orientation
role: Make the disappearance of sign under squaring feel like double reversal canceling.
prompt_core: Illustrate a left-pointing directed segment undergoing the same flip-and-scale action twice, with the first flip sending it across the origin and the second returning orientation to positive while magnitude is squared. The emotional tone should be clarity: direction information cancels, magnitude survives and compounds. Clean symmetry choreography, almost dance-like.
caption: Squaring erases orientation because two flips cancel.

source_key: 8.4 Historical note: the uneasy status of negatives
title: Historical note: the uneasy status of negatives
role: Show that negatives were long treated as suspect because their meaning was thin.
prompt_core: Create a restrained historical scene of an early algebraist confronting a negative solution on paper while practical contexts—debts, directions, bookkeeping—hover nearby as the settings that eventually made negatives intelligible. The tone should be thoughtful, not mocking. Candlelight, paper, ink, quiet conceptual tension.
caption: Negatives felt suspicious until structure gave them a home.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. title: Thought experiments
role: Invite quick mental rehearsal of flip, undo, and double-flip.
prompt_core: Make a sparse sequence with a single arrow on a line, a mirror-like origin, and two successive flips traced in faint arcs. A second tiny panel can show a debt/gain analogy or east/west motion for reinforcement. The point is instant mental replay, not spectacle.
caption: Count the reversals and watch the sign take care of itself.
